item.title: David Tolu Young named Fiji flag bearer, Anahira McCutcheon baton carrier for Commonwealth Games
item.description: Fijian swimming sensation David Tolu Young will be the flag bearer, while Anahira McCutcheon has been selected as the baton bearer for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland. This has been confirmed by Team Fiji as at hletes and officials presented their traditional i-tatau to Minister for Youth and Sports Jese Saukuru today.
item.content:encoded:
Fijian swimming sensation David Tolu Young will be the flag bearer, while Anahira McCutcheon has been selected as the baton bearer for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland.

This has been confirmed by Team Fiji as at hletes and officials presented their traditional i-tatau to Minister for Youth and Sports Jese Saukuru today.

Speaking at the ceremony, Saukuru congratulated the athletes, coaches, managers, officials and support staff selected to represent Fiji.

Team Fiji consists of 58 athletes and officials, with athletes competing across athletics, basketball, boxing, lawn bowls, judo, para-athletics, swimming and weightlifting.

Saukuru says 19 female athletes are part of the team, and their achievements continue to inspire young Fijians to dream bigger.

He told the athletes that they have earned the privilege of wearing the Fiji badge through dedication, perseverance and strong performances.

Saukuru says the Government remains committed to supporting sport through stronger coaching, athlete development, sports science and high-performance programmes.

Saukuru also reminded them that when they wear Fiji’s colours, they carry the hopes, dreams and prayers of Fijians at home and abroad.

He is urging them to trust their preparation, support one another and compete with courage and confidence.

He encouraged the athletes to compete with passion and integrity and represent Fiji with pride and humility, both on and off the field.

Meanwhile, Team Fiji’s Chef de Mission Ajay Ballu says preparations have included medical assessments and workshops covering Team Fiji values, responsibilities, travel, accommodation, competition schedules, safeguarding, anti-doping, hydration, mental health and nutrition.

The team’s medical support group includes three physiotherapists and one doctor.

The first major group is expected to leave Fiji on 19th July, while some athletes are travelling earlier for overseas training before joining the team in Glasgow.

The para-athletics team is expected to depart tomorrow morning ahead of the Games.

The 23rd Commonwealth Games are taking place in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2, 2026.

item.title: Seruvakula calls for discipline ahead of England Test
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/07/2026 FIJI Water Flying Fijians coach Senirusi Seruvakula says his side must be more disciplined in both attack and defence if they are to cause another upset against England this Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/07/2026

FIJI Water Flying Fijians coach Senirusi Seruvakula says his side must be more disciplined in both attack and defence if they are to cause another upset against England this Sunday.

Speaking from Liverpool, Seruvakula says last week's defeat was very disappointing.

He says the team believed they started well in the first half, but costly mistakes eventually led to their downfall.

Seruvakula adds that the match highlighted where Fiji currently stands when competing against Tier 1 nations.

Insert: Seruvakula on Fiji, 8th July 2026

The Flying Fijians head into the match expecting a tough challenge from England, who have won 10 of the 11 previous meetings between the two sides.

However, Fiji will draw confidence from their historic 30-22 victory over England at Twickenham in 2023—their first-ever win over the English—and will be aiming to repeat that achievement.

The Flying Fijians will take on England at 1.10am Sunday at Hill Dickinson Stadium.

In other international fixtures this weekend, New Zealand will face Italy at 5.05pm Saturday at Sky Stadium on Saturday, Australia will host France at 7.40pm Saturday in Brisbane, and Ireland will take on Japan at 10.10pm Saturday.

On Sunday, South Africa will play Scotland at 3.10am in Pretoria , while Wales will face Argentina at 7.10am in San Juan.

item.title: M-PAiSA launches first-ever sponsorship venture to back grassroots women’s netball
item.description: M-PAiSA has launched its first-ever sponsorship venture under Digital Financial Services Pte Limited, backing a nationwide Netball Fiji Rally aimed at identifying grassroots talent and creating pathways for young girls to reach national and international levels. While announcing the sponsorship, the General Manager of Digital Financial Services Pte Limited, Shailendra Prasad says the company wante
item.content:encoded:
M-PAiSA has launched its first-ever sponsorship venture under Digital Financial Services Pte Limited, backing a nationwide Netball Fiji Rally aimed at identifying grassroots talent and creating pathways for young girls to reach national and international levels.

While announcing the sponsorship, the General Manager of Digital Financial Services Pte Limited, Shailendra Prasad says the company wanted its first major sponsorship commitment to support grassroots women’s sport and communities across the country.

He says M-PAiSA is a homegrown service that has reached hundreds of thousands of Fijians, particularly at the grassroots level, and supporting women’s netball was a natural fit.

He says 50 percent of M-PAiSA users are female, with women playing a major role in managing household finances, supporting their families, running small businesses and keeping local economies moving.

The nationwide rally will reach the Western, Northern and Central Eastern regions, involving districts including Suva, Nasinu, Tailevu and Naitasiri, communities from Sigatoka to Rakiraki, as well as Labasa, Savusavu, Bua and Taveuni.

He says the initiative is designed to take opportunities directly to grassroots players and give young girls a genuine platform to showcase their talent.

The General Manager says Fiji has seen many men build successful international careers through rugby and rugby league, and the question now is why women should not have similar opportunities through netball.

He says the rally could become a launching pad for a young girl in a rural village to be discovered, rise through the national system and eventually carve out a professional career internationally.

He says M-PAiSA wants Fijian netballers to believe that competing in professional competitions such as the ANZ Premiership and Suncorp Super Netball is within their reach.

Beyond professional pathways, he says the initiative is also focused on keeping young girls active, healthy and focused while providing mentorship, building teamwork and giving them a positive outlet away from negative social pressures.

M-PAiSA is supporting the rally in partnership with its parent company, Vodafone Fiji, which has a long history of supporting sport in the country.

The General Manager says just as homegrown M-PAiSA rose to national prominence by positively impacting and empowering thousands of women across Fiji, the company hopes the sponsorship can help netball achieve similar growth.

He also pointed to Cape Verde as an example of how a relatively small country can gain international recognition through sport, saying Fiji can also take its netball to greater heights by identifying raw talent and nurturing it.

He says the court now belongs to players across the Western, Northern and Central Eastern divisions, and M-PAiSA is proud to support their journey.

item.title: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties 4-3 and will face Argentina in quarter-finals
item.description: Switzerland have booked a quarter-final clash against defending champions Argentina at the FIFA World Cup after beating Colombia 4-3 on penalties in their Round of 16 match. The match ended nil-all after full time and extra time, with both sides unable to find the back of the net.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland have booked a quarter-final clash against defending champions Argentina at the FIFA World Cup after beating Colombia 4-3 on penalties in their Round of 16 match.

The match ended nil-all after full time and extra time, with both sides unable to find the back of the net.

Switzerland held their nerve in the penalty shootout to edge Colombia 4-3 and keep their World Cup campaign alive.

Meanwhile, Argentina staged a stunning comeback to beat Egypt 3-2 in their Round of 16 clash and captain Lionel Messi has also broken two World Cup records.

Messi now holds the record for the most goals scored by a player at the World Cup with 21, and has also become the first player to score in nine consecutive World Cup matches.

Meanwhile, the quarter-finals starts this Friday with France taking on Morocco at 8am, and Spain will face Belgium at 7am on Saturday.

On Sunday, Norway will take on England at 9am while Argentina will face Switzerland at 1pm.

item.title: Nawaqanitawase to start for NSW Blues in Game 3
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/07/2026 Mark Nawaqanitawase will line-up again for the NSW Blues when they face the Queensland Maroons in Game 3 of the State of Origin tonight.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/07/2026

Mark Nawaqanitawase will line-up again for the NSW Blues when they face the Queensland Maroons in Game 3 of the State of Origin tonight.

Nawaqanitawase was brought in by NSW coach Laurie Daley for Game 2 where he scored a try, and he will be starting on the wing.

Other big names included in the Blues squad for tonight include James Tedesco, Stephen Crichton, Mitchell Moses, Nathan Cleary, Payne Haas, Hudson Young, and Isaiah Yeo.

Meanwhile, the Maroons has the services Kalyn Ponga, Robert Toia, Jojo Fifita, Cameron Munster, Harry Grant, Kurt Capewell, Reece Walsh and Murray Tuilagi.

The Blues won Game 1, edging the Maroons 22-20, while the Maroons defeated the Blues in Game 2 44-24.

The Blues will face the Maroons at 10.05 tonight.

item.title: Eseta Waqaira replaces injured Kalesi Tawake in Fiji Pearls squad
item.description: Eseta Waqaira has been named as a replacement for Kalesi Tawake in the Fiji Pearls squad for the upcoming Spirit Series in Sydney. In a statement, the Fiji Netball Association thanked Tawake for her hard work, commitment, and dedication to the team and wished her a smooth and speedy recovery.
item.content:encoded:
Eseta Waqaira has been named as a replacement for Kalesi Tawake in the Fiji Pearls squad for the upcoming Spirit Series in Sydney.

In a statement, the Fiji Netball Association thanked Tawake for her hard work, commitment, and dedication to the team and wished her a smooth and speedy recovery.

Waqaira now joins the Fiji Pearls as they continue preparations for the Spirit Series in Sydney.

The Association has wished Waqaira all the best as she joins the Fiji Pearls, expressing confidence that she will embrace the opportunity and give her best in the upcoming Spirit Series.

The Navolau, Naitasiri player, plays goal defence and goal keeper.

item.title: Father’s rugby journey inspires 21-year-old Amy Peters
item.description: 21-year-old Suva Women’s Number 8 Amy Peters says her love for rugby was inspired by her father, former Tailevu and Suva player Lagi Peters, as she prepares for the upcoming Subrails Marama Championship. While speaking with fijivillage News, Peters, who is from Wailevu in Kadavu, says she grew up around rugby, and the sport naturally became part of her life.
item.content:encoded:
21-year-old Suva Women’s Number 8 Amy Peters says her love for rugby was inspired by her father, former Tailevu and Suva player Lagi Peters, as she prepares for the upcoming Subrails Marama Championship.

While speaking with fijivillage News, Peters, who is from Wailevu in Kadavu, says she grew up around rugby, and the sport naturally became part of her life.

She says her father’s involvement in rugby played a major role in developing her passion for the game.

Inse rt: Peters sharing about her father 7th July 26

Peters says her father played for Tailevu and Suva and was also believed to have been part of an extended Fiji squad.

The 21-year-old has been part of the Suva Women’s team since 2021.

Speaking about preparations for the upcoming tournament, Peters says the team has been working hard over the past few weeks, with a strong focus on improving their techniques.

She says the coaches have also played an important role in helping the players improve, and she believes preparations are progressing well.

On discipline, Peters says it comes down to how players prepare themselves for a match.

She says if players respect themselves and the game, they will also respect other players on the field.

Peters says the team has not faced many major challenges and thanked Suva supporters for their continued backing.

She hopes supporters will turn up at the ground and get behind the Suva Women’s team during the tournament.

The Skipper Cup and Subrails Marama Championship kicks off next month.

Meanwhile, the Ranadi Cup starts this Friday with Bua taking on Taveuni at 11am at Bua College, Yasawa will face Vatukoula at 11am at Churchill Park, Northland will play Ra at 11am at Ratu Cakobau Park, Coastland will meet Northern Bulls at 11am at Narauyaba Sports Ground, Navosa will meet Ovalau at 11am at Buckhurst Ground and Malolo will meet face Yasawa at 3pm at Lawaqa Park.

The Vanua Cup also kicks off this Friday with Bua taking on Macuata at Bua College, Yasawa will meet Vatukoula at Churchill Park, Northland will face Ra at Ratu Cakobau Park, Coastland will meet Northern Bulls at 11am at Narauyaba Sports Ground, Nasinu will take on Ovalau at Buckhurst Ground and Namosi will meet Serua at Thompson Park.

All matches are at 3pm with the U20 starting at 11am.

item.title: 17-year-old Gianna Singh eyes Fiji and US representation as she chases golfing dream
item.description: At just 17 years old, Gianna Singh aspires to represent both Fiji and her country of birth, the United States, in golf and become

item.description: Lionel Messi has broken two FIFA World Cup records after inspiring defending champions Argentina to a stunning 3-2 comeback victor

item.description: The Queensland Maroons and the New South Wales Blues will go into tonight's State of Origin decider unchanged, with the series set

item.description: Defending champions Argentina have beaten Egypt 3-2 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash after staging a stunning comeback.Eg

item.title: Argentina and Egypt clash for quarter-final spot
item.description: Two of world football's biggest stars will go head-to-head tomorrow morning when Lionel Messi's Argentina take on Mohamed Salah's

item.title: Saint John's College U18 Raluve team eyes trophy for Levuka
item.description: The Saint John's College Under-18 Raluve team is determined to take the Weet-Bix Raluve trophy to Levuka this year.Expressing his
